name: Goma Tei Ramen (Ward)
tagline: A local mini-chain that consistently beats out the trendy newcomers — the sesame-rich tonkotsu broth is what keeps Honolulu coming back.
mustOrder: Sesame ramen — rich ground sesame broth, the signature bowl that defines this place
Spicy miso ramen — complex broth with layered heat
Gyoza — crispy pan-fried dumplings, perfect starter
parking: Ward Centre parking structure, validated with purchase
talkStory: Goma Tei originated in Sapporo, Japan and brought its signature sesame-based tonkotsu to Honolulu in 2005 — the 'goma' in the name literally means sesame. The ground sesame broth is their proprietary recipe and you genuinely cannot get it anywhere else on the island. Now with multiple Oahu locations, the Ward spot stays packed with locals who consider it their everyday ramen over trendier newcomers. Customize your noodle firmness and spice level at ordering — weekday lunch beats the dinner rush.
